Tests that Math.pow(1, -Infinity) returns NaN.

# Purpose
This code is a single assertion test that checks the behavior of the `Math.pow` function in JavaScript. It verifies that raising the number `1` to the power of negative infinity (`-Infinity`) results in `NaN` (Not-a-Number). The use of `assert` ensures that the test will fail if the result is not `NaN`, indicating a deviation from expected behavior. This test provides narrow functionality, focusing specifically on the mathematical operation involving `Math.pow` with a base of `1` and an exponent of `-Infinity`.
